name: Summer Community of Practice - the university
designIntent: A flexible multi-agent planning workflow for designing, launching, facilitating, assessing, and closing out a summer Community of Practice at the university. Nodes are semantic planning units for coding or coordination agents, not rigid runtime primitives.

nodes.label: COP project starts
nodes.purposeInstructions: Begin the Summer Community of Practice design process for the university and the user. Treat this workflow as an agentic planning artifact that can be revised as new constraints, stakeholders, or institutional priorities appear.

nodes.label: Step 0 kickoff brief
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create the initial project brief with the user. Capture the purpose of the Community of Practice, target audience, expected summer timeline, institutional context, known constraints, decision rights, and what success should look like.

nodes.label: Charter agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft a one-page COP charter. Include working title, audience, goals, scope boundaries, session cadence, expected participant outcomes, sponsor expectations, dependencies, risks, and open questions for the user.

nodes.label: Discovery fan-out
nodes.purposeInstructions: Launch discovery agents in parallel. Each agent should gather enough information to inform program design without waiting for the other discovery tasks unless a dependency is explicit.

nodes.label: Stakeholder needs agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Identify participant groups, likely needs, motivations, barriers, and stakeholder expectations. Consider university faculty, staff, administrators, instructional support partners, and any units the user wants included.

nodes.label: Calendar constraints agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Map the summer schedule constraints. Identify academic calendar dates, likely vacation periods, campus events, room availability, holidays, and realistic meeting cadence options.

nodes.label: Resource intake agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Inventory resources needed and available: budget, rooms, Zoom support, food, incentives, facilitator time, student support, communications help, technology access, and administrative support.

nodes.label: Existing assets scan
nodes.purposeInstructions: Collect prior materials that can accelerate the COP: workshops, slides, readings, templates, AI guidance, university policies, participant examples, previous program artifacts, and reusable evaluation items.

nodes.label: Success metrics agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Define what evidence would show that the COP worked. Propose participation metrics, engagement indicators, artifact outputs, learning outcomes, confidence changes, application evidence, and reporting needs.

nodes.label: Risk and access agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Identify risks and inclusion requirements early. Check accessibility, scheduling equity, workload burden, privacy issues, AI policy concerns, communication clarity, and backup plans for low enrollment or staff capacity limits.

nodes.label: Discovery synthesis
nodes.purposeInstructions: Merge the six discovery streams into one design-ready synthesis. Produce key constraints, design implications, unresolved decisions, and recommended COP strategy options for the user.

nodes.label: Strategic blueprint agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create the COP strategic blueprint. Include audience definition, program promise, goals, learning arc, session sequence, facilitation model, communications strategy, assessment plan, logistics assumptions, and decision list for the user.

nodes.label: Blueprint approval loop
nodes.purposeInstructions: Use this loop to review and revise the COP blueprint until the user is comfortable using it as the basis for detailed build work.

loop.intent: Repeat review and revision until the user approves the COP blueprint.
loop.body: Send blueprint to the user, collect feedback, resolve open decisions, and revise the blueprint.
loop.exitRule: Exit when the user approves the blueprint or explicitly authorizes build work to begin.
loop.agentNotes: This is a human approval loop. Do not treat the first draft as final. Preserve open questions and decision rationale.

nodes.loopDefinition: Repeat review and revision until the user approves the COP blueprint.

exitConditions.text: the user approves the COP blueprint
the user authorizes build work with known open issues

nodes.label: the user blueprint review
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pause for the user's review. Collect comments on goals, audience, session structure, scope, constraints, language, and any institutional sensitivities.

nodes.label: Revise blueprint
nodes.purposeInstructions: Revise the blueprint from the user's feedback. Update assumptions, decisions, program scope, and risks. Return the revised version to the approval loop.

nodes.label: Build workstreams
nodes.purposeInstructions: After blueprint approval, launch the major build workstreams in parallel. Each workstream should produce concrete artifacts that can be integrated into the launch-ready COP package.

nodes.label: Learning outcomes agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Write practical learning outcomes for the COP. Make them specific enough to guide curriculum design and assessment, but flexible enough for a community-centered program.

nodes.label: Curriculum map agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Design the sequence of sessions. For each session, specify purpose, topic, activity structure, pre-work, facilitation notes, participant artifact, and connection to the overall learning arc.

nodes.label: Session design sprint
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft the actual session plans. Include run-of-show, prompts, activities, slides/materials list, facilitator moves, discussion prompts, and outputs participants should produce.

nodes.label: Curriculum review loop
nodes.purposeInstructions: Review and refine the curriculum until it is coherent, feasible, and aligned with the user's goals.

loop.intent: Iterate curriculum drafts until the user or designated reviewers approve them.
loop.body: Review outcomes, session plans, activities, readings, and participant artifacts; then revise.
loop.exitRule: Exit when the curriculum package is approved for launch.
loop.agentNotes: Keep revision history and do not over-polish before strategic approval. Prioritize coherence and facilitator usability.

nodes.loopDefinition: Iterate curriculum drafts until approved for launch.

exitConditions.text: Activities are feasible for the summer cadence
the user approves the curriculum package

nodes.label: Curriculum review
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pause for review of curriculum package. Reviewers should check clarity, relevance, workload, sequencing, and fit with university context.

nodes.label: Revise curriculum
nodes.purposeInstructions: Revise outcomes, session plans, activities, readings, templates, and facilitation notes based on review feedback. Return the package to the curriculum loop.

nodes.label: Schedule and space agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create the operational schedule. Identify session dates, times, locations or Zoom links, room needs, setup needs, calendar holds, and backup options.

nodes.label: Registration ops agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Design the registration flow. Create registration fields, confirmation messages, waitlist logic, roster tracking, attendance capture, and privacy-conscious data handling notes.

nodes.label: Participant comms ops
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft the operational communication sequence: confirmation, calendar invitation, pre-session reminders, session follow-ups, resource links, and missed-session notes.

nodes.label: Logistics readiness loop
nodes.purposeInstructions: Iterate operational readiness until logistics are credible enough for launch.

loop.intent: Repeat logistics readiness checks until operational blockers are resolved.
loop.body: Check schedule, room or Zoom, registration, reminders, materials, accessibility, and support ownership.
loop.exitRule: Exit when no launch-blocking logistics gaps remain.
loop.agentNotes: Surface unresolved blockers clearly rather than hiding them in prose.

nodes.loopDefinition: Repeat logistics readiness checks until no launch-blocking gaps remain.

exitConditions.text: Registration and roster process is ready
Accessibility and support needs are addressed

nodes.label: Ops readiness check
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pause for logistics review by the user or the operational owner. Confirm dates, spaces, registration, communication timing, and support responsibilities.

nodes.label: Resolve logistics gaps
nodes.purposeInstructions: Resolve readiness issues discovered in review. Update owners, timelines, registration details, communications, accessibility items, and contingency plans.

nodes.label: Audience segmentation agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Define outreach segments and motivations. Segment likely participants by role, interest, AI readiness, summer availability, and relevance to COP outcomes.

nodes.label: Message strategy agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Develop the outreach value proposition. Explain why the COP matters, what participants will gain, expected time commitment, session format, and how it supports university priorities.

nodes.label: Outreach channel agents
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create outreach assets in parallel for several channels. Each channel agent should adapt the same core value proposition to its audience and format.

nodes.label: Email copy agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft launch email, reminder email, and final call email. Include subject lines, concise body copy, registration link placeholder, and audience-specific variations.

nodes.label: Partner outreach agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft short outreach notes for campus champions, department leaders, centers, and support units. Ask them to share the opportunity and identify likely participants.

nodes.label: Web calendar copy agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft web listing, calendar blurb, short newsletter copy, and social or internal announcement copy. Keep language clear, practical, and aligned with university tone.

nodes.label: Marketing package merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Merge all outreach assets into one campaign package. Check consistency of dates, registration links, title, value proposition, tone, accessibility, and calls to action.

nodes.label: Campaign review loop
nodes.purposeInstructions: Review marketing assets until they are accurate, compelling, and approved for release.

loop.intent: Repeat outreach review until campaign assets are approved.
loop.body: Review emails, partner notes, web copy, and registration language; then revise.
loop.exitRule: Exit when the user and any communications reviewer approve launch materials.
loop.agentNotes: Keep the core message stable while adapting for different channels.

nodes.loopDefinition: Repeat outreach review until campaign assets are approved.

exitConditions.text: Dates, links, and audience are accurate

nodes.label: Comms review
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pause for review of the campaign package. Confirm title, audience, promise, dates, registration link, distribution plan, and any institutional language requirements.

nodes.label: Revise campaign assets
nodes.purposeInstructions: Revise campaign assets from review comments. Keep all channels consistent and ready for coordinated launch.

nodes.label: Assessment design agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Design the assessment approach. Define baseline questions, session pulse checks, final evaluation, artifact review strategy, and what evidence will be useful to the user and university stakeholders.

nodes.label: Pre-survey agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create the pre-survey or intake form. Capture participant goals, current practices, confidence, constraints, project interests, and consent or privacy considerations if applicable.

nodes.label: Pulse and final eval agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create short pulse check items for each session and a final evaluation. Balance useful evidence with low participant burden.

nodes.label: Reporting plan agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Design the reporting template. Include participation, themes, artifacts, examples of application, feedback, recommendations, and next-cycle decisions.

nodes.label: Workspace setup agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create or specify the shared workspace. Prepare folders, naming conventions, participant resource hub, facilitator files, draft agenda storage, survey links, and artifact submission locations.

nodes.label: Templates agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create participant-facing templates: project planner, reflection sheet, peer feedback form, session worksheet, example artifact format, and implementation checklist.

nodes.label: Facilitator guide agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Create facilitator guide and run-of-show details. Include timing, prompts, transitions, discussion management, contingency moves, accessibility reminders, and post-session tasks.

nodes.label: Partner mapping agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Identify campus partners, possible guest contributors, and units that should be aware of the COP. Clarify who should be invited to contribute, endorse, or amplify.

nodes.label: Guest confirmations
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pause for any guest speaker, partner, or stakeholder confirmations. Confirm availability, role, topic, timing, and preparation needs.

nodes.label: Guest integration agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Integrate confirmed guests or partners into the session plan, communications, materials, and facilitator guide. Keep guest contributions aligned with participant outcomes.

nodes.label: Build package merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Merge all completed workstreams into a launch-ready COP package. Confirm curriculum, logistics, marketing, assessment, workspace/materials, and partner contributions fit together.

nodes.label: Integrated program plan
nodes.purposeInstructions: Produce the integrated COP launch plan. Include timeline, owners, session package, outreach calendar, registration process, assessment instruments, risk log, and decision points.

nodes.label: Final launch review
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pause for the user's final launch review. Confirm that the program is ready to open registration and begin outreach.

nodes.label: Launch ready?
nodes.purposeInstructions: Decision gate. True means the COP is ready for launch. False means blockers remain and should be resolved before outreach or registration opens.

nodes.label: Fix launch blockers
nodes.purposeInstructions: Resolve any issues found in final review. Update the integrated plan, owners, dates, materials, outreach assets, registration details, or risk controls before returning to final review.

nodes.label: Launch execution
nodes.purposeInstructions: Run launch tasks in parallel once the plan is approved. These agents should coordinate timing so the public invitation, registration, participant resources, and facilitator prep align.

nodes.label: Open registration
nodes.purposeInstructions: Open the registration mechanism, verify confirmation messages, test the form, and begin roster tracking.

nodes.label: Send outreach
nodes.purposeInstructions: Send or stage launch communications. Coordinate email, partner distribution, web/calendar listing, and reminder schedule.

nodes.label: Publish materials hub
nodes.purposeInstructions: Publish or prepare the participant resource hub. Confirm links, permissions, document naming, accessibility, and placeholder materials for future sessions.

nodes.label: Facilitator prep
nodes.purposeInstructions: Prepare facilitators for launch. Review run-of-show, participant list, facilitation roles, timing, tech checks, prompts, and contingency plans.

nodes.label: Launch status merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Merge launch execution status. Confirm registration is open, outreach is underway, materials are available, and facilitation prep is complete.

nodes.label: Session delivery loop
nodes.purposeInstructions: Run this loop once for each COP session in the summer sequence. Each cycle prepares, delivers, captures evidence, and improves the next session.

loop.intent: For each scheduled COP session, prepare, facilitate, collect feedback, and adjust the next session.
loop.body: Prepare session packet, facilitate the live session, capture artifacts and feedback, then update the next session plan.
loop.exitRule: Exit when all scheduled summer COP sessions are completed.
loop.agentNotes: The concrete implementation may treat the session list as an array. Each cycle should update the running program log.

nodes.loopDefinition: For each scheduled COP session, prepare, facilitate, capture feedback, and improve the next session.

exitConditions.text: All scheduled sessions have been delivered
Session artifacts and feedback have been captured

nodes.label: Prepare session packet
nodes.purposeInstructions: Prepare the next session packet. Confirm agenda, slides, activities, templates, participant reminders, tech needs, and facilitator notes.

nodes.label: Facilitate live session
nodes.purposeInstructions: Human delivery point. Facilitate the COP session, manage discussion, support participant work, and adapt to the room while preserving the session purpose.

nodes.label: Capture feedback
nodes.purposeInstructions: Capture attendance, participant artifacts, pulse check responses, notable questions, implementation ideas, barriers, and follow-up needs from the session.

nodes.label: Adjust next session
nodes.purposeInstructions: Use feedback and facilitator notes to adjust upcoming sessions. Update prompts, examples, pacing, reminders, and participant support items before the next cycle.

nodes.label: Closeout fan-out
nodes.purposeInstructions: After all sessions finish, launch closeout agents in parallel to synthesize evidence, preserve artifacts, and recommend next steps.

nodes.label: Outcome synthesis agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Synthesize what happened. Summarize participation, session completion, artifacts produced, examples of participant application, and evidence aligned with the original success metrics.

nodes.label: Feedback analysis agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Analyze pre-survey, pulse checks, final evaluation, comments, and facilitator observations. Identify themes, tensions, participant needs, and improvement opportunities.

nodes.label: Archive handoff agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Organize final artifacts, templates, slides, recordings if any, session notes, participant resources, and reusable materials. Prepare a clean archive for future COP cycles.

nodes.label: Next-cycle recommendations
nodes.purposeInstructions: Recommend whether and how to continue the COP. Suggest changes to audience, cadence, format, curriculum, marketing, assessment, and institutional support.

nodes.label: Closeout merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Merge outcome synthesis, feedback analysis, archive handoff, and next-cycle recommendations into one reporting package.

nodes.label: Final report agent
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft the final report for the user and university stakeholders. Include executive summary, goals, process, participation, evidence, artifacts, lessons learned, risks, and recommended next steps.

nodes.label: the user final review
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pause for the user's review of the final report and closeout package. Capture requested changes and final decisions.

nodes.label: Report accepted?
nodes.purposeInstructions: Final decision gate. True means the report and archive are acceptable. False means revise the report or closeout artifacts and return to the user for review.

nodes.label: Revise final report
nodes.purposeInstructions: Revise the final report, archive, recommendations, or evidence summary based on the user's feedback. Return to final review.

nodes.label: COP complete
nodes.purposeInstructions: The Summer Community of Practice planning, delivery, assessment, and closeout process is complete. Final outputs are ready for the user and future reuse.
